steps.title: Use the closest possible values
steps.content:
To make the means as close as possible, make data set A as small as possible and data set B as large as possible.
  • For A, use the lower endpoint of each interval: $20,30,40,50$.
  • For B, use the largest integer in each interval: $19,29,39,49$.

steps.title: Find the smallest possible sum difference
Convert the sum difference to a mean difference
steps.content:
Each data set has $23$ integers, so divide the smallest possible sum difference by $23$:
$$\frac{23}{23}=1$$
That matches choice B, so B is correct.
